description_long: This object is a vintage fax machine with a boxy, rectangular form and a beige exterior. The front panel features a numeric keypad, a digital display, and several red indicator buttons labeled 'DIAL' and 'REDIAL', as well as a label reading 'AT-5000'. The top surface includes two visible tape reels under a transparent cover, a rotary dial, and a large circular speaker grille. There are also several switches and buttons, some with red tops, and a sloped section that houses the keypad and display. A corded telephone handset is mounted on the right side, resting in a cradle with visible push buttons for operation. The overall design is utilitarian, with grouped controls and clear labeling for user interaction.
description: A beige, boxy vintage fax machine with a numeric keypad, tape reels, a speaker grille, and a corded handset mounted on the side.
description_view_0: Side view showing the sloped control panel, tape reels, and the speaker grille, with the handset partially visible on the right.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the numeric keypad, digital display, red indicator buttons, and the 'AT-5000' label.
description_view_2: Opposite side view focusing on the handset cradle, red buttons, and the speaker grille, with the control panel sloping away.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the flat back, the speaker grille, and the tape reel compartment, with minimal visible controls.
attribute_control_panel_layout: The control panel is sloped and located on the front upper surface, featuring a numeric keypad on the right, a digital display and red indicator buttons in the center, and a rotary dial and tape compartment on the left. The speaker grille is positioned on the top right, with additional switches and buttons grouped near the handset cradle.
attribute_paper_tray_design: No distinct paper tray is visible; the design suggests paper may be fed through a slot or compartment integrated into the sloped front panel, but no adjustable guides or covers are apparent.
attribute_output_slot_location: The output slot is not clearly visible, but it is likely integrated into the front lower section near the keypad and display, with no external guides or trays apparent.
attribute_handset_attachment: A corded telephone handset is mounted on the right side of the machine, resting in a molded cradle with visible push buttons for operation and a coiled cord connecting it to the main body.
